item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article could not be sourced. Thus, it is assumed that the image is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) and has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the interviews but the wish fulfilment of this bothers me. No vote.

item.title: Triggers my phobia of marionettes
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, RealDolls, mannequins, marionettes: all are incredibly disturbing to me. That personal note aside, this reminds me of the Stepford Marriage Rings — SCP-288.

This SCP is a partner who is as devoted, possessive, and eager to please as the characters in many songs in stories, and that is what makes it incredibly scary. Frankly, the sentiments that this SCP expressed in the interviews are eerily similar to things I have heard from real people in my family who have been involved in abusive relationships. The scale of this entry is very, very human.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Admittedly, I can see why the rewrite was an improvement on the original. I really loathe the "sex doll but REAL" concept unless something really different and creative is being done with it, and this wasn't really different or creative enough— particularly the body of the description. "Her" responses in the logs were surprisingly the most intriguing aspect of the article, but there weren't enough of them/enough in interesting situations. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: IMO the combination of the fact that the agents were concentrating on taking out the rogue D-class before the SCP was damaged, and that they were dealing with a sex-doll, meant they were taken a bit by surprise… once it grabbed onto someone's neck, I figured it could easily cause asphyxiation, since there aren't any vital areas to take out. It absorbed ten rounds and was still conversational after the whole thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am not much of a fan of the concept, but the interviews were pretty good… at least up until the doll killed an agent despite any actual ability to pull something like that off

inb4 echo says it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 for successfully pulling off yandere with a sex doll.

item.title: Re: I'm not doing a major revision since it's already at -10...
item.content:encoded: This catches me a lot more now. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Cool, the pic I suggested worked pretty well.

I was kinda meh-ish before the rewrite, but now it's really well done.

+1

item.title: +5 After Approx. -11
item.content:encoded:
Looks like the rewrite was a success.

Crazyking begins a dubstep rave

item.title: Re: I'm not doing a major revision since it's already at -10...
item.content:encoded:
Out of deletion territory, though.

You may actually salvage this. Something I've never seen with a removal and rewrite. At least not firsthand.

item.title: Re: I'm not doing a major revision since it's already at -10...
item.content:encoded: Apparently this was an improvement. The rating has gone +7 since the edit. Still underwater, but there's some hope for it now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvote though i would really love to see that it stays a simple sex doll, even after entering the active phase. Making it unable to produce complex movement due to the lack of most joints would be creepy as hell.

item.title: Re: I'm not doing a major revision since it's already at -10...
item.content:encoded: The new picture is MUCH better, and I like the revised & amped psychopathy. Upvote.

item.title: I'm not doing a major revision since it's already at -10...
item.content:encoded:
But I did a few tweaks to see what happens:

  • Changed the picture
  • Added some uncanny valley fuel in the lines "The object shows considerable wear, with its original pigmentation faded or missing over roughly 35% of its surface." and "Its speech is clear and comprehensible, but it appears unable to alter either volume or inflection."
  • And upped the psycho by rewriting following:

SCP-1805 attempts to acquiesce to any expressed desire of the imprinted subject regardless of its nature, though actions seem subject to SCP-1805's understanding. SCP-1805 has upon occasion taken metaphorical or idiomatic expressions literally. SCP-1805 is also extremely possessive and will not permit the subject to leave SCP-1805’s presence, or vice-versa. SCP-1805 will react violently to any attempts to separate them, and will likely cause harm to the subject, itself, or others. SCP-1805 also will show extreme negative reaction to any suggestion that the subject does not reciprocate SCP-1805’s affection, or that the subject shares affection— or even substantial interest— for any other object or individual. This not only applies to current actual sexual interest by the subject, but to any sign of affection, interest or attention, current, historical or hypothetical. SCP-1805 has shown violent rages when subjects have expressed positive feelings for past girlfriends, pets, children, parents, and fictional characters. SCP-1805’s devotion to an imprinted subject appears to subside upon death of the subject.

  • UPDATE: Realized I had edited out the "healing" from the description. Rather than add it back in, I revised the last bit of the 2nd addendum:

SCP-1805 remained in an active state for ten days after the termination of D-12787, the longest time so far recorded. In that time, all gross physical damage to SCP-1805 repaired itself through an unknown mechanism. Repaired material showed additional loss of pigmentation and signs of wear. When SCP-1805 entered an inactive state, it was placed back in storage.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Holy fuck. That would actually make me reconsider my vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And don't forget the Hello Kitty Abortion Panties.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Suggested pic if it survives
From the SCP fuel files

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'll admit, it is hard to take a sex-doll SCP seriously.

But this isn't that bad at all. Hell, the last testing log gives an interesting mental image of how badly it looks now.

And you guys have to remember, there's an Atomic-powered dildo and Rule_34.exe. If those can get a pass, why not this?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
OH GOD

it's a realdoll that acts like my EX

Shitting bricks here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I do think this is salvageable… And I'd explain how, but Doktori already did (in my opinion) down below.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It just seems too perfect a doll-it essentially becomes a living person, like that movie Mannequin with Kim Cattrell. To me, a less perfect sex doll with limited abilities trying to act like a regular human-that would be creepier. Just my opinion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not super-huge on the concept, but its well written and interesting. Upvote, if only out of sympathy for an idea done well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I find it interesting that this is tanking so quickly. I've had much more ill-conceived SCPs that lingered in single digits until I self-deleted them. Seems this time I picked something that rubs a lot of people the wrong way1, which makes me wonder if this is salvageable. Judging from the initial reaction, this looks like a concept that's doomed regardless of execution.

Footnotes
1. Pun? What pun?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Awww, this is at -6. Too bad; I liked it. :(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't care for this most of the way through, but the interview logs were very well-written, and I enjoyed the manner the thing talks in.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCPs inspired by anime tend to go very, very badly…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think the concept shows a lot of promise, but the general creepiness factor is a little understated.You could really kick it up a notch if instead of being a perfectly obedient, subservient wife, she was the anime 'yandere' stereotype - Sweet and loving at first, then it devolves into obsessive, violent behavior. More info on 'Yandere' Good job so far!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Enh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I found it suitably creepy. +1

item.title: Re: #1
item.content:encoded: Oh, I was picking up on some "kill the baby/pet/coworker/mailman that's stealing my beloved!" vibes, too. But it should be made more explicit, yes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This just doesn't catch me.

Allright, that goddamn picture…

Now I'm thinking of it hopping in a car…chasing you down to a convenience store…ugh. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted, although I really think this needs changes to make it less wish-fulfillment and more SCP-creepy. How about these for some ideas:

1. or that the subject shares affection for any other individual. It doesn't just refer to other sexual partners…..

2. Lower quality of 'doll. I'm obviously not an expert on the topic but I'd assume that lower quality ones would fall even further into the uncanny valley then this does. Make it less, "vaguely attractive assuming you are insane enough to get recruited as a class-D" and more "WTF would anyone regardless even think of sleeping with that thing."

3. Isn't very mobile. Doesn't have jointed fingers for example. Why would it need them for it's normal usage? Just more attempts uncanny valley-ize this thing.

4. Ineffective take on "orders." For example if class-D personal says "I hate that guy" in regards to the doctor testing him the SCP starts going crazy and trying to kill the doctor. Or if someone gets a hangover "I wish I was dead…." happy to oblige. Not very effectively as it can't move much of it's body but still….

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Kind of a related concept to the Stepford Marriage Rings, but one-sided. I get what you're trying for, with the "perfect girlfriend" who just so happens to be bug-nuts crazy, but it's not doing anything for me. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually like it. Takes the beautiful concept of marriage and turns it into so much more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Consider it a placeholder. I can't really search for a more appropriate image at work, given the subject matter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I noticed that too!
…
Shit, we've memorized the face of a sex doll…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Locked Box is test is not, and has never been, conclusive.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Can't help but notice that you're using an image from the wikipedia article, which is a bit… unsubtle.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Huh. It fits the 'definition' of Euclid, yeah (sapience/free will). But the old Locked Box test says it's safe.
I'll let you call on it, being author and all. No sense picking apart the object class.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: gaaaaah

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Am I allowed to second this?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Actually, that is sort of intentional. I mean, that's one of the reasons I find the RealDoll concept creepy to begin with. I just took that creepy feeling and ran with it.

i.e. What if the looser with one of those things wished real hard that it was his very own slave girl, and he got his wish, and the slave girl was as fucked in the head as he was.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree. It's sapient and subject to unpredictable sapient action, and that means what it'll do is unpredictable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is… I can't bring myself to like it. Even though I want to know better, I can't help but feel vibes of juvenile wish fulfillment here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I believe standard practice (in my head-cannon at least) is that anything showing free will is automatically Euclid because of potential unpredictability.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hah, it's a RealDoll waifu. This is too perfect. Upvote.
Although this last test log:

SCP-1805 was involved in a near containment breach when the imprinted test subject D-12787 attempted to take SCP-1805 hostage. D-12787 threatened SCP-1805 with [REDACTED] a solvent apparently smuggled from [REDACTED]. D-12787 demonstrated [REDACTED] melting 30% of the silicone flesh off of SCP-1805's left forearm. SCP-1805 allowed this to occur as D-12787 made demands for [REDACTED], [REDACTED], and a private plane.

This simply has too much [REDACTED] for my liking. That's really the only issue I have.

Also, why's it Euclid? As long as nobody screws it, there's no chance of it activating, right?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Second attempt at a humanoid SCP.
